ref: Rebekah later told Isaac, “Those Hittite wives of Esau are making my life miserable! If Jacob marries a Hittite woman, I'd be better off dead.”
text: Na Rebɛka sele Yisake kyɛ, “Hiti mmrasua nemɔ amaa wiase afono me. Sɛ ɔkɔba kyɛ Yakob gya Hiti mmrasua nemɔ bie kyɛ Yisɔ deɛ nemɔ a, ye de né nwuli po a ahaa ɔte paa.”
translated: Then Rebecca said to Isaac, "I am weary of my life because of the Hittite women. If Jacob takes a wife from the Hittite women like these, from the daughters of the land, what good will my life be to me?"

ref: and the muscle on his hip joint had been injured. That's why even today the people of Israel don't eat the hip muscle of any animal.
text: Yiti ɔbaadwu ɛnɛ ye, Yisraelfoɛ nni ndii bɔ ɔsɔ adwonguɛ so ne, ofikyɛ abɔfo ne bɔle Yakob taku so ndii so.
translated: And it came to pass, when Israel dwelt in that land, that Reuben went and lay with Bilhah his father's concubine: and Israel heard it.

ref: Her husband is a prophet. Let her go back to him, and his prayers will save you from death. But if you don't return her, you and all your people will die.
text: Yiti sá fa biaa n’aye kɔma ye: ɔte ngɔmhyɛlɛniɛ, na okoli wɔ ti dwirɛ kɔma wɔ maa ekonya ngoa. Nakoso sɛ amva ye angɔ amma ye a, ye de kaye kyɛ ewue yeɛ ekowu ɔ, ɛne wɔ nwo ama ne wɔ nyɛmmoa mukoraati.”
translated: If we do not take good care of our children: the future generation, and invest in their education and well-being, then what we are saying is that we don't care if they perish, and that we have no need for helpers in the future.

ref: and I'll ask one of them for a drink. If she gives me a drink and then offers to get some water for my camels, I'll know she is the one you have chosen and that you have kept your promise to my master.
text: Taluwa bɔ ngɔhã ngohyire ye kyɛ, ‘Mesrɛ wɔ, ka wɔ bua ne ase ma nno nzue ne bie,’ ná ye wora ɔkɔha kyɛ, ‘No bie, na ngɔma w’afupɔngɔ nemɔ koso bie’ nen, maa ɔyɛ sona bɔ aneanea nu aye ye ama w’akoaa Yisake ɔ. Na ɛhe kɔmaa ngonwu ye kyɛ ekuro me mene dwirɛ paa.”
translated: I begged my father, saying, ‘Please, intercede for me so that I might not be childless,’ but he answered me, ‘You have no child because you have despised your husband and treated him with contempt,’ and this is the reason he gave me to my master Isaac. And that is why I have come to fall at your feet and seek your mercy.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Na Yakob nwuni bɛ ne, osele kyɛ, “Nyameɛ asraafoɛ mɔ ɛhe!” Yiti ɔtole berɛ dumaa Mahanaim.
translated: When Jacob saw them, he said, "This is the camp of God!" So he named that place Mahanaim.
